extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, whose condition is (polypharmacy AND peptic_ulcer AND hyperkalemia AND pregnancy AND elderly AND hepatic_impairment). rec_drug_b is set true only by r3, whose condition is just polypharmacy. Since r2's condition includes polypharmacy as a conjunct, any input that makes r2 fire (rec_drug_a = true) necessarily also satisfies r3 (rec_drug_b = true). Both r2 and r3 have priority 50 and are the sole rules for their respective outputs, so no priority/tie/default interaction overrides them. Therefore whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is forced true, violating the mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Counterexample: set all six of r2's conjuncts true (low_ejection_fraction irrelevant, set false). Then r2 yields rec_drug_a=true and r3 yields rec_drug_b=true, so both are jointly true.
